key_insight: PriorProof turns proof novelty into a time-relative, machine-computable score by measuring how surprising a Lean proof’s dependency footprint is compared with an earlier snapshot of Mathlib.
engineering_relevance: This offers a decomposable signal for formal proof search, proof analysis, and technique detection without requiring a hand-built ontology or human-labeled training data.
method: Extract the proof-term dependency footprint from each Lean theorem.
Score that footprint using weighted surprisal under a retrieval-conditioned, hierarchically smoothed prior built from an earlier Mathlib snapshot.
Learn statement retrieval from proof-derived contrastive pairs, then evaluate against blinded human and LLM judgments.
evidence_signal: In blinded evaluation, PriorProof matched the majority of domain raters on 53/76 pairs (69.7%) and showed endpoint-calibration behavior, with accuracy rising to 84.2% in the largest score-gap bin.
watch_item: Because the metric is anchored to an earlier library snapshot, results may depend on snapshot choice and on how well retrieval generalizes across theorem domains and evolving proof styles.
paper_title: PriorProof: A Point-in-Time Measure of Technique Novelty for Formal Proofs
